title: populus × canadensis f. marilandica
intro: Populus × canadensis f. marilandica, also known as the black cottonwood or eastern black cottonwood, is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America. The bark of this tree has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been utilized to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Populus × canadensis f. marilandica include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to soothe skin irritations, coughs, and colds.
